codes.title: Jujutsu Legacy Codes
codes.intro: Codes are mainly useful for rerolling the character systems that matter most in Jujutsu Legacy: techniques, clans, and race-related progression. Redeem working codes for Technique Spins and Clan Spins, reach Level 5 first, then open the in-game menu and enter the code in the redeem box.
codes.redeemTitle: How to redeem Jujutsu Legacy codes

expiredGroups.label: Expired milestone codes
expiredGroups.note: Former milestone rewards, mostly Technique Spins, Race Spins, or Clan Spins

expiredGroups.label: Expired update and event codes
expiredGroups.note: Former update, apology, and event rewards
expiredGroups.codes: Infinity, Hanami, Jackpot, BloodManipulation, DisasterFlames, Higuruma, Comedian, Ishigori, RaceSpin2, SorryShutdown, Gifts, SorryForGiveSpecialClan, SorryForInfBugs, Krampus Sukuna, Cat Gojo, WChristmas, Christmas1

redeemSteps: Reach Level 5 before trying to redeem codes.
Press M or click the Menu button on the left side of the screen.
Enter the code in the redeem box.
Press OK or Enter to claim the reward.

officialLinks.title: Jujutsu Legacy Trello and Discord Links
officialLinks.intro: This hub keeps the useful Jujutsu Legacy links in one place so players can play the Roblox experience, join the official community, check the Discord server, and open the Trello board without digging through fake invites and outdated boards.

links.label: Jujutsu Legacy Trello Board

beginnerGuide.title: Jujutsu Legacy Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Jujutsu Legacy gives new players several systems at once: techniques, clans, vessels, spins, and leveling. The safest early route is to learn the menu, unlock code redemption at Level 5, and use spins on systems that actually shape your build instead of spending them at random.

steps.title: Launch the official Roblox experience
steps.description: Open Jujutsu Legacy from the Roblox game page and start from the current live experience. The official Roblox description lists the current Level Cap as 2000.
steps.goal: Get into the correct experience before following any guide or code list.

steps.title: Learn the menu first
steps.description: Use the Menu button or M key to access core options, including the code redeem box. This menu is where new players start noticing the build systems in Jujutsu Legacy.
steps.goal: Know where codes and character systems are before spending spins.

steps.title: Reach Level 5 before redeeming codes
steps.description: Code redemption requires Level 5 in Jujutsu Legacy. Defeat early enemies and complete starter progression until the redeem box accepts codes.
steps.goal: Unlock free Technique Spins and Clan Spins as early as possible.

steps.title: Use spins with a build plan
steps.description: Technique Spins reroll your cursed technique, while Clan Spins target clan progression. Save larger spin batches for serious reroll attempts instead of spending one code at a time.
steps.goal: Avoid replacing a useful rare technique too early.

steps.title: Pick a first technique target
steps.description: The current technique pool in Jujutsu Legacy includes Limitless, Rika, Cursed Manipulation, Sky Manipulation, Ice Formation, Disaster Flames, Ten Shadows, Idle Transfiguration, Mythical Beast Amber V2, Ten Shadows V2, and Heian Shrine.
steps.goal: Aim for a technique that helps with grinding, bosses, or PvP.

steps.title: Understand vessels as a separate system
steps.description: The current vessels in Jujutsu Legacy are Sukuna, Ishigori, Uro, Mechamaru, and Kashimo. Treat vessels as a separate power layer from techniques when planning your build.
steps.goal: Do not confuse a good technique roll with a complete setup.

steps.title: Check Discord and Trello before major rerolls
steps.description: Use Discord for codes and announcements, and Trello for structured mechanics reference before spending a large spin stack in Jujutsu Legacy.
steps.goal: Make reroll decisions with current game information.

techniqueTierList.title: Jujutsu Legacy Technique Tier List
techniqueTierList.intro: This Jujutsu Legacy technique tier list focuses on which cursed techniques are worth keeping when you spend Technique Spins. Higher tiers favor strong late-game value, boss utility, PvP pressure, and flexible use across more than one mode.

tiers.label: Best reroll targets
tiers.summary: Keep these techniques for serious progression, bossing, and PvP-focused builds in Jujutsu Legacy.

tiers.label: Strong keeps
tiers.summary: Excellent techniques worth building around before chasing the hardest late-game rolls.

tiers.label: Good and usable
tiers.summary: Solid techniques that carry early and mid-game progression, usually replaced by stronger rare or V2 options later.

tiers.label: Starter or niche picks
tiers.summary: These work while leveling, but are not the main reason to spend a large Technique Spin stack.

spinsClanAndRaceRerolls.title: Jujutsu Legacy Spins, Clan Rerolls, and Race Rerolls
spinsClanAndRaceRerolls.intro: Spins are a main progression resource in Jujutsu Legacy. Technique Spins change your combat kit, Clan Spins adjust clan-based stat identity, and Race Spins affect long-term stat direction, so each one should be treated as a build decision rather than a throwaway button press.

rows.affects: Cursed Technique selection and your main skill kit.
rows.bestFor: Chasing a core combat style such as Limitless, Rika, Cursed Manipulation, Sky Manipulation, Disaster Flames, Ten Shadows V2, or Heian Shrine.
rows.plan: Spend after deciding whether the account aims for boss farming, raid damage, mobility, or a vessel-linked setup.

rows.affects: Clan identity and passive build value.
rows.bestFor: Tuning a finished or nearly finished account after the technique and vessel direction are set.
rows.plan: Save Clan Spins until the main technique is stable, then reroll for a clan that supports the chosen stat plan.

rows.affects: Race-based stat profile and long-term scaling direction.
rows.bestFor: Finishing a build that already has a clear role, such as sword damage, technique damage, or vessel damage scaling.
rows.plan: Use Race Spins later than Technique Spins because race value depends on the final build target.

rows.affects: Free reroll stock from official Jujutsu Legacy code releases.
rows.bestFor: Saving a large batch of rerolls before making a major build reset.
rows.plan: Redeem available code rewards first, then reroll in one planned session so you can stop once the target is reached.

vesselsAndBuilds.title: Jujutsu Legacy Vessels and Builds
vesselsAndBuilds.intro: Jujutsu Legacy lists Sukuna, Ishigori, Uro, Mechamaru, and Kashimo as current vessels. Each vessel is best understood as a build anchor: the vessel matters most when it connects cleanly with a technique path, boss farm target, or late-game raid plan.

vessels.name: Sukuna
vessels.focus: Aggressive damage, domain pressure, and late-game vessel scaling.
vessels.tip: Pair Sukuna planning with technique storage and reroll saving before a full account reset.

vessels.name: Ishigori
vessels.focus: Direct ranged pressure and heavy burst windows for boss farming.
vessels.tip: Prioritize stable damage stats and boss-farm consistency before chasing cosmetic rewards.

vessels.name: Uro
vessels.role: Spacing and technique synergy vessel path
vessels.tip: Keep Sky Manipulation in mind before spending Technique Spins, because Uro value is tied to that style.

vessels.name: Mechamaru
vessels.focus: Mechanical pressure, transformation value, and Puppet Manipulation upgrade routing.
vessels.tip: Do not judge Mechamaru only by the base vessel slot; its value comes from the full Puppet Manipulation setup.

vessels.name: Kashimo
vessels.focus: Fast pressure, aggressive movement, and lightning-based burst damage.
vessels.tip: Save enough rerolls before chasing Kashimo-linked setups, because partial lightning builds feel weaker than a finished one.

questsBossesAndLevelingRoute.title: Jujutsu Legacy Quests, Bosses, and Leveling Route
questsBossesAndLevelingRoute.intro: The current Jujutsu Legacy level cap is 2000, so the safest route is to move through quest bands instead of wandering between bosses at random. Use quests for steady experience, then switch into boss and raid farming once the account has enough damage, health, and a stable technique-vessel setup.

bands.area: Jujutsu High and early Shibuya routing
bands.targets: Starter quests, early curse enemies, Takuma Ino route
bands.route: Build a usable technique first, complete nearby quests for repeatable experience, and avoid farming rare drops before the account can clear basic enemies quickly.
bands.priority: Unlock reliable damage and movement before chasing swords or accessories.

bands.area: Shibuya approach and connected quest paths
bands.targets: Eso route, graveyard-side quests, pre-Metro leveling
bands.route: Stay on quest chains that are close together and only move forward once enemies stop taking too long to defeat.
bands.priority: Increase stats, stabilize the main technique, and prepare for Metro bosses.

bands.route: Use the Metro band as the main bridge from basic leveling into boss-focused progression. Choso and Mahito are common checkpoint bosses before higher Metro targets.
bands.priority: Farm experience first, then return for specific drops once clears are consistent.

bands.route: The Culling Games route introduces more valuable boss targets and sword-related farming. JudgeMan is especially important for sword collection routes.
bands.priority: Start tracking boss drops, but keep leveling if kill speed is poor.

bands.route: This band matters for vessel paths and late-game builds. Uro and Ishigori are especially relevant because both connect directly to vessel planning.
bands.priority: Choose whether the account farms vessel unlocks, sword drops, or raw experience before repeating bosses.

bands.targets: Kashimo, Sukuna-related bosses, Strongest Raid, Divine General Raid
bands.route: At this point, build quality matters more than raw route order. Enter raids with a stable technique, vessel plan, accessory direction, and enough stats to survive repeated waves.
bands.priority: Farm raids for build-defining drops only after the account can clear late-game bosses reliably.

raidsDropsSwordsAndAccessories.title: Jujutsu Legacy Raids, Drops, Swords, and Accessories
raidsDropsSwordsAndAccessories.intro: After the beginner stage, Jujutsu Legacy progression shifts from simple leveling into targeted farming. Bosses and raids such as Megumi, JudgeMan, Uro, Ishigori, Kashimo, and late-game raid sources become important because their rewards can unlock swords, vessels, accessories, traits, races, or fighting-style upgrades.

drops.value: Good early sword target for players moving beyond basic quest leveling.
drops.bestFor: Sword collection and Ten Shadows-related progression planning.

drops.rewards: Playful Cloud, Inverted Spear of Heaven
drops.value: Useful weapon farm for players who want stronger melee options before deeper Culling Games farming.
drops.bestFor: Sword users and players building a broader weapon collection.

drops.value: High-priority Culling Games target because both listed rewards are weapon upgrades tied to the JudgeMan route.
drops.bestFor: Players who want rare swords and a clear Culling Games farming objective.

drops.value: Important for players planning Uro Vessel and Sky Manipulation-related progression.
drops.bestFor: Sky Manipulation users and Uro-focused builds.

drops.value: Core farm for players who want to unlock or build around Ishigori Vessel.

drops.rewards: Kashimo Head, Electric Pole, Thunder Spirit
drops.value: Strong late-game target for players building lightning pressure or Kashimo Vessel routes.
drops.bestFor: Mythical Beast Amber and rushdown-focused accounts.

drops.rewards: Nah I'd Win, Honored One, Strongest of Today, Six Eyes
drops.value: Late-game raid target for players seeking build-defining prestige and trait rewards.
drops.bestFor: Advanced accounts with enough damage and survivability for repeated raid clears.

drops.rewardTypes: Fighting style, Accessory, Title, Trait, Race item
drops.rewards: Imperial Curse Arts, Sukuna Heian Haori, King of Curses, Strongest in History, Demonic Usurpation, Heian Emperor
drops.value: One of the most important late-game raid farms because its reward pool connects directly to Sukuna-style and high-scaling builds.
drops.bestFor: Sukuna-focused players, raid farmers, and endgame progression accounts.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Jujutsu Legacy

questions.question: What is Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: Jujutsu Legacy is a Roblox anime RPG fighting experience where you master cursed techniques, collect vessels, fight bosses and raids, and use codes for Technique and Clan Spins.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: Reach Level 5 in Jujutsu Legacy, press M or click the Menu button, enter a working code in the redeem box, and press OK to claim Technique Spins or Clan Spins.

questions.question: What is the level cap in Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: The current Jujutsu Legacy level cap is 2000, and the official Roblox page lists this directly in the game description.

questions.question: What are Technique Spins in Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: Technique Spins reroll your cursed technique in Jujutsu Legacy, so you should save larger batches for serious reroll attempts instead of spending them one at a time.

questions.question: Where can I play Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: You can play Jujutsu Legacy on Roblox through the official experience page, and the official Roblox community and Discord server share codes and announcements.

questions.question: Which vessels are available in Jujutsu Legacy?
questions.answer: The current Jujutsu Legacy vessels are Sukuna, Ishigori, Uro, Mechamaru, and Kashimo, and each one pairs best with a matching technique path.
